title: Unstage a file (when you added it by mistake)

content.problemStatement:
Sometimes you run `git add ` and then realise - \
**“Wrong file added in staging area”**. \
In that case, **you can remove the file from the staging area without losing your changes**.

---

**Git Command:**
```sss
git restore --staged 
```

---

**What this command does**:
- Removes the file from staging → it won’t be part of the next commit.
- Keeps your changes safe → your edited file in the working directory stays exactly the same.
- Only the staging area is cleaned, nothing is deleted.
